howItWorks.intro: This caffeine half-life calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is caffeine half-life?
faq.answer: Caffeine half-life is about 5 hours for most adults. This means if you drink 200mg, you still have 100mg in your system 5 hours later. It varies by genetics, age, and liver function.

faq.question: How does caffeine affect sleep?
faq.answer: Caffeine consumed within 6 hours of bedtime significantly disrupts sleep quality, even if you fall asleep fine. The rule of thumb: stop caffeine 8-10 hours before bed.

faq.question: How much caffeine is in common drinks?
faq.answer: Coffee (8oz): 95mg. Espresso: 63mg. Black tea: 47mg. Green tea: 28mg. Cola: 34mg. Energy drink: 80-300mg. Caffeine pills: usually 200mg.

faq.question: Can I build caffeine tolerance?
faq.answer: Yes — regular consumption reduces its stimulant effects. A 1-2 week break (caffeine fast) resets tolerance. Withdrawal symptoms (headache, fatigue) last 2-9 days.
